beats.title: SIEGE NIGHT
beats.subtitle: Defense in Depth · The Full Picture
say: Every gap, every shortcut, every forgotten door — I will find it. Tonight, I bring everything. Every vector, every door, all at once.
Not tonight. Tonight, every guardian answers. Welcome to Siege Night.

beats.title: SIEGE NIGHT
beats.subtitle: All twenty guardians · The Risk Management Framework
say: One attacker, many weapons. One city, twenty guardians. This is defense in depth.

beats.title: THE CITY STANDS AS ONE
say: The Null strikes everywhere at once. Watch the city answer.

beats.title: The assault — repelled, vector by vector
bullets: Phishing email → AT, the trained human spots it
Stolen credential → IA proves identity; AC-6 limits the reach
Tailgating the door → PE stops the walk-in
Malware on a host → SI detects and cleans it
Wiping the logs → AU-9 protects the record
say: Each weapon meets a guardian. No single failure ends the city.
Repelled — this time. I just mapped every lock you have. Next time, I bring the right keys.

beats.title: ...and still it holds
bullets: Counterfeit part → SR checks authenticity
Eavesdropping the wire → SC encrypts it
The departing insider → PS revoked the keys
Ransomware detonates → CP restores from clean backup
Unpatched crack → RA found it, SI fixed it
say: Layered defense means no single failure ends the fight. If one layer fails, others still prevent, detect, limit, or recover.

beats.title: The Risk Management Framework, end to end
say: Here is how it all comes together, the lifecycle from 800-37.
Prepare, categorize, select, implement, assess, authorize, and monitor, forever.

beats.title: How to actually use 800-53
bullets: 1. Categorize the system — FIPS 199, with help from SP 800-60
2. Select a baseline from SP 800-53B, then tailor it
3. Implement the controls in the system
4. Assess them — procedures in SP 800-53A
5. Authorize to operate, then 6. Monitor continuously
say: So in the real world, where do I start?
Prepare, categorize the system, select and tailor a baseline, implement, assess, authorize, and never stop watching.

beats.title: Your bookshelf
bullets: SP 800-53 — the control catalog (this series)
SP 800-53B — the control baselines
SP 800-53A — how to assess the controls
SP 800-37 — the Risk Management Framework
FIPS 199 / 200 — categorize and set minimum requirements
say: These books travel together. Know which one answers which question.

beats.title: Twenty guardians, one shield — their oaths
bullets: AC: None pass unknown; none hold more than they need.
AU: Every footstep written; no page erased.
RA: I do not guess the storm; I measure it.
SC: Filter at the edge; cipher on the wire.
CP: We rehearse the dark, so we survive it.
say: Hear the oaths together, and the whole defense fits in your hand.

beats.title: GUARDIAN ROLL CALL
beats.subtitle: Five questions across the whole city
say: Final test. Five questions, all twenty guardians. Ready?

beats.q: Which guardian proves you are who you claim, before access?
say: One. Who checks your identity at the gate?
beats.why: Identification and Authentication proves identity before access is granted.

beats.q: Where do the Low, Moderate, and High control baselines live?
say: Two. Where do the baselines live?
beats.why: The baselines live in companion volume 800-53B.

beats.q: Ransomware hits and encrypts everything. What brings you back?
say: Three. What saves you from ransomware?
beats.why: CP-9, System Backup, is what brings you back after encryption.

beats.q: Which document tells you HOW to assess whether controls work?
say: Four. Which book holds the assessment procedures?
beats.why: SP 800-53A is the assessment companion — it tells you HOW to test controls.

beats.q: A senior official accepts the risk and lets the system operate. Which control?
say: Five. Who signs to let the system go live?
beats.why: CA-6, Authorization, is the official's formal acceptance of risk.

beats.title: The siege breaks — but the war isn't won
bullets: You met all 20 families across six layers of defense
You can read any control: ID, Control, Discussion, Related, Enhancements
But knowing the guardians isn't the same as standing up a system the right way
beats.kicker: THE SIEGE ENDS · THE CAMPAIGN BEGINS
say: You walked every wall, every tower, every vault. The siege broke on our walls, Nova.
...but I left with the blueprints. I'll be back — and next time, it won't be one city.
Then how do we prove a NEW citadel is safe before we trust it with the Crown Data?
That, apprentice, is a campaign. And in Act Two, you're going to lead it.

beats.title: THE CAMPAIGN
beats.subtitle: Next: standing up a real system with the Risk Management Framework
say: The guardians are known. Now you learn to deploy them — categorize, select, implement, assess, authorize, and watch. The campaign begins in Act Two.
